key_messages_to_land.rationale: Operations leaders need to demonstrate clear payback periods and use conservative assumptions to secure budget approval and manage stakeholder expectations

product_requirements.requirement: Unified operational dashboard with real-time metrics across channels, WFM, and QA in a single view
product_requirements.example_quote: Maven aims for a unified agent performance dashboard, currently relying on multiple tools (Looker, Zendesk, WFM, QA) for different metrics

product_requirements.requirement: Automated knowledge gap identification that analyzes tickets and suggests articles to create or update
product_requirements.example_quote: Discover analyzes existing knowledge bases and tickets to identify information exclusively in tickets but not in the knowledge base, suggesting new articles or enhancements with potential ticket coverage

product_requirements.requirement: Flexible usage-based pricing with clear visibility into what consumes automated resolutions vs. action credits
product_requirements.example_quote: Brad requests a detailed breakdown of resolution usage across the essential bot, advanced bot, email auto-reply, and email suggestions, along with a forecast up to December 29th

product_requirements.requirement: API rate limits that scale with enterprise volume requirements without constant upgrade requests
product_requirements.example_quote: DistroKid is hitting current API rate limits (700 requests per minute) and needs an upgrade to avoid issues and enable new functionalities

product_requirements.requirement: Bulk license management tools with weekly active agent lists for monthly optimization
product_requirements.example_quote: Amy requests a process to receive a list of active agents weekly to perform monthly clean-ups and manage license usage more effectively

product_requirements.requirement: Business rule audit and optimization tools to review and consolidate extensive triggers, automations, and macros
product_requirements.example_quote: Denise highlights the need to review Carousell's extensive business rules (805 active macros, 445 triggers), noting only 12

product_requirements.requirement: Native integrations with workforce management systems for seamless routing and scheduling
product_requirements.example_quote: Andrés guides Chris on how to troubleshoot 'no work stream' or 'multiple work streams' issues in Workforce Management by checking ticket details and conditions

product_requirements.requirement: Proactive anomaly detection with alerting for queue depth, SLA breach risk, and unusual patterns
product_requirements.example_quote: Jason explained the new real-time monitoring dashboard, highlighting its omnichannel view, integration of WFM/QA metrics, and features like anomaly detection and a monitoring assistant for proactive operations

product_requirements.requirement: Multi-source knowledge integration supporting SharePoint, Confluence, Google Drive, and internal wikis
product_requirements.example_quote: A significant pain point is the internal knowledge base (Confluence) being outside Zendesk, having poor search capabilities, and leading to agents struggling to find correct information

product_requirements.requirement: Intent-based routing that automatically triages and assigns tickets based on content analysis
product_requirements.example_quote: Maven has successfully implemented Intelligent Triage and sentiment analysis

product_requirements.requirement: Detailed migration planning tools with delta migration capabilities and SLA protection during cutover
product_requirements.example_quote: Tracie will provide SLA details and emphasizes the need for precise timing in the cutover plan to ensure operational staff can cover SLAs during the transition

product_requirements.requirement: Custom reporting fields for operational metrics like first interaction resolution and client health status
product_requirements.example_quote: Pathwise wants a checkbox or dropdown to track FIR, defined as an issue resolved within a single interaction timeframe (even if multiple messages), distinct from Zendesk's 'one-touch ticket' definition

product_requirements.requirement: Granular role-based permissions beyond basic admin/agent distinctions for operational team structures
product_requirements.example_quote: Billy highlighted Zendesk's move towards more granular role-based permissions, allowing customization of access and editable profile details beyond just admin/agent roles

product_requirements.requirement: Automated ticket merging and duplicate detection to reduce agent manual cleanup work
product_requirements.example_quote: Kaylin inquires about an automatic ticket merging feature for customer emails, expressing concern about potential incorrect merges if fully automated

information_sources.source: Internal vendor workshops and platform demos
information_sources.notes: Operations leaders rely heavily on direct vendor demonstrations tailored to their specific workflows and use cases to evaluate capabilities

information_sources.source: Peer customer references and case studies
information_sources.notes: Leaders value hearing how similar organizations solve operational problems and want to understand shared challenges with other customers
Operations leaders attend vendor events like Relate conference to learn about new features, roadmap direction, and network with other customers

information_sources.source: Internal champion and technical team assessments
information_sources.notes: Rely on their technical teams to evaluate integration complexity, conduct proof-of-value testing, and provide implementation feasibility assessments

information_sources.source: Vendor account team and customer success managers
information_sources.notes: Expect proactive guidance from dedicated account teams on optimization opportunities, new feature adoption, and resolving operational challenges
Used by operations teams for troubleshooting specific technical issues and understanding configuration options

information_sources.source: Analytics and usage reports from current platform
information_sources.notes: Heavily analyze their own operational data to identify trends, justify investments, and measure ROI of platform features

messaging_preferences.preference: Data-driven demonstrations with live operational metrics and real-world examples
messaging_preferences.example_quote: Tessa presented performance data from May 25 to end of April, noting increasing new tickets and an 82% zero-touch resolution rate, indicating efficient handling through automation and self-service

messaging_preferences.preference: Phased implementation approaches with clear milestones and ROI at each stage
messaging_preferences.example_quote: Stephanie outlines future phases: Phase 2 involves API integrations for more automation (targeting 60%+ AR), and Phase 3 includes multilingual support and new channels (targeting 80% AR)

messaging_preferences.preference: Transparent pricing breakdowns showing per-unit costs and flexible contract options
messaging_preferences.example_quote: Brett requests the 49K minutes cost to be divided by 125 users to establish an all-in per-user per-month (PPM) cost for telephony

messaging_preferences.preference: Implementation scoping with detailed timelines, resource requirements, and responsibility matrices
messaging_preferences.example_quote: Harvey.AI requested specific owners and detailed timings for each step to ensure smooth orchestration

messaging_preferences.preference: Conservative ROI models with realistic assumptions and clear payback period calculations
messaging_preferences.example_quote: David suggested a conservative ROI approach, emphasizing that even with high-cost tickets, the system, particularly the 'crawl' phase, should demonstrate significant self-payment

messaging_preferences.preference: Regular operational reviews with account teams focusing on optimization and feature adoption
messaging_preferences.example_quote: Ciara agrees to regular syncs, suggesting preferred times between 4 PM and 6 PM Dublin time due to her existing US calls

messaging_preferences.preference: Access to specialized technical resources for complex integrations and configuration guidance
messaging_preferences.example_quote: Josh reports that initial Zendesk resources for the Advanced Agent team were not ideal, leading to manager Eric swapping in more tech-savvy individuals like Kelly to supplement Shristi

competitive_landscape.context: Considered too clunky and complex for specific use cases, lacking internal expertise to manage effectively, with some organizations actively seeking to avoid multi-year renewals
Significantly cheaper pricing creates competitive pressure, with migration complexity and data quality issues during transitions
Used as standalone AI automation solution with integration concerns, pricing comparisons, and evaluation of whether to continue or consolidate into Zendesk platform
Incumbent telephony provider being evaluated for replacement, potentially on month-to-month contracts making switching more feasible
Current contact center solution being considered for phased transition to Zendesk Contact Center
Current platform with custom UI for customer portals being evaluated against Zendesk for modernization

pain_points.description: CX Executives face intense pressure to justify technology investments and renewals, often needing to absorb cost increases within static or shrinking budgets while demonstrating ROI to CFOs and boards.
pain_points.example_quote: Budget Constraints: Sarah expressed budget constraints, noting a potential $81,000 increase, and Stephanie clarified that early renewals typically require a minimum $50,000 uplift

pain_points.description: Leaders need to handle increasing customer volume and expand to additional brands or regions without proportionally increasing agent headcount, requiring efficiency through technology and automation.
pain_points.example_quote: Strategic Scaling: Ashley articulated her team's strategy to scale through efficiency, automation, AI, and technology rather than adding more people, seeking the 'next best step' to achieve this

pain_points.description: Executives struggle with vendor compliance requirements that threaten service continuity, face legal risks from non-compliant usage, and must navigate complex license audits with limited IT resources.
pain_points.example_quote: Non-Compliance Warning: Giselle informed Joanne about an email from Zendesk's Head of Sales regarding Merchantrade's non-compliant environment, which prevents renewal, and the legal team's readiness to audit

pain_points.description: CX leaders must rapidly implement systems to meet new regulatory requirements for complaint tracking, resolution reporting, and consumer satisfaction measurement with tight deadlines.
pain_points.example_quote: Regulatory Compliance (BNM): Joanne reveals a new public policy from Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM) effective 2025 (enforcement this year) requiring regular review of complaint resolution, reporting on common issues, resolution turnaround, and consumer satisfaction (CSAT)

pain_points.description: Executives inherit or operate multiple disconnected instances, manual spreadsheet-based workflows, and separate logins that create operational inefficiency and prevent unified reporting to leadership.
pain_points.example_quote: Current Manual Process: Ashley details their current inefficient process involving spreadsheets, Teams dialing, and manual ticket creation/transcription for 3,000 outbound calls per quarter

pain_points.description: Leaders face pressure to quickly evaluate, test, and demonstrate ROI from AI investments while managing internal nervousness and competing vendor claims about AI capabilities.
pain_points.example_quote: Group One's AI Evaluation: Alexander states Group One needs more AI for customer service, but their evaluation of Zendesk's Auto Assist was not impressive, leading them to explore other solutions like Fin

pain_points.description: CX Executives discover their current systems lack necessary data protection controls for sensitive information, creating compliance risks and requiring urgent remediation for healthcare-adjacent or regulated industries.
pain_points.example_quote: Sparrow's Current Data Handling: Cameron explained that Sparrow currently has no specific data protection in Zendesk, agents can see all tickets, and FMLA forms/certifications containing sensitive data are downloaded and uploaded to their product

pain_points.description: Executives navigate complex multi-party relationships between direct vendors, implementation partners, and integrators, often inheriting inefficient arrangements that increase costs without adding value.
pain_points.example_quote: Direct Contract Request: Niki's company (ACC Premier, supporting Constellation Brands) wants to transition from contracting Zendesk through partner Faye to contracting directly with Zendesk

goals.description: Consolidate multiple instances, brands, or systems into unified platforms that eliminate duplicate work, reduce licensing costs, and provide single-pane-of-glass visibility for leadership reporting.
goals.example_quote: Consolidation Goal: The primary goal is to consolidate the Rush Limbaugh instance into the main iHeartRadio instance to streamline operations, eliminate separate logins, and have a single annual renewal

goals.description: Implement AI capabilities that demonstrably reduce operational costs, handle high-volume interactions, and free agents for complex work while maintaining quality standards and customer satisfaction.
goals.example_quote: Low Touch Rate & AI Potential: Nima points out that 20% of tickets are closed with just one reply, suggesting an opportunity to use AI for frequently asked questions to achieve cost savings

goals.description: Expand proven CX solutions across newly acquired companies, additional brands, or merged organizations while maintaining efficiency and leveraging existing technology investments.
goals.example_quote: 2026 Scaling Strategy: The primary goal for 2026 is to scale existing Zendesk operations to include 2-3 additional brands from Compass International Holdings, growing the capacity of their current setup

goals.description: Leverage CX data and satisfaction metrics to identify at-risk customers, reduce churn, and demonstrate the revenue impact of customer experience investments to executive leadership.
goals.example_quote: Sarah's Objectives: Sarah is evaluating customer success platforms (To Tango, Churn Zero) and wants to leverage Zendesk for satisfaction scores (CSAT), sentiment tracking, and escalation data to improve retention and reduce operational costs

goals.description: Replace spreadsheet-based tracking and manual processes with integrated workflows that reduce administrative burden and improve speed while maintaining compliance and audit trails.
goals.example_quote: Automation Goal: Ashley emphasizes the need for automation and simplification to move away from legacy systems and heavy reliance on spreadsheets, which was a core reason for adopting Zendesk

goals.description: Obtain concrete data on agent workload, productivity, and breaking points to make defensible decisions about headcount, budget requests, and resource allocation to the board or CEO.
goals.example_quote: Workload Capacity & Workforce Management: Ashley expresses a need for data-driven insights to determine consultant workload capacity and breaking points for headcount planning

goals.description: Shift from reactive ticket handling to proactive outreach that prevents issues, confirms critical information, and demonstrates customer care value through integrated calling and SMS.
goals.example_quote: CCaaS Use Cases: Emily and Cameron identified potential CCaaS use cases like updating leave dates, confirming return-to-work dates, and birth confirmations, especially via SMS

objections.objection: Cost increases exceeding budget without clear ROI
objections.example_quote: Budget Limit: Luis states he can rationalize the $6,000 increase due to the non-recurring discount, but going significantly beyond $200,000 (e.g., to $215,000) would likely not work for his team

objections.objection: Vendor lock-in and inability to switch platforms
objections.example_quote: Customer's Ultimatum: Joanne indicates that if Zendesk cannot accommodate their request, she will be forced to seek another vendor and migrate services

objections.objection: Insufficient time for implementation and internal alignment
objections.example_quote: Time Constraint: Joanne emphasizes the short timeframe until the contract ends in June, making it difficult for her IT team to plan for a 150-license implementation without an interim solution

objections.objection: AI capabilities not meeting expectations or competitive alternatives
objections.example_quote: Market Perception of Zendesk: Alexander highlights a market perception that Zendesk is blocking other AI tools, which he acknowledges Colin denies, but notes this perception is problematic

objections.objection: Pricing model complexity and hidden costs
objections.example_quote: AR Pricing Discrepancy: Luis expects the additional ARs to be priced at $1, based on previous negotiations and budgeting, while Zendesk's current proposal is $1.50

objections.objection: Migration complexity and loss of historical data
objections.example_quote: Metrics and Reporting Impact: Migrated tickets lose their original created/closed dates and first-time reply metrics, requiring separate reporting or custom fields for historical data, which can be a showstopper for some clients

objections.objection: Professional services adding unnecessary cost without clear value
objections.example_quote: EAC (Premier Access) Value: Luis emphasizes that his team does not see value in the EAC (Premier Access/professional services component) and prefers to invest any additional spend into more ARs

objections.objection: Compliance requirements creating operational restrictions
objections.example_quote: Intercom's Non-Compliance: Intercom initially failed to comply with Zendesk's security requirements, leading to restrictions on new connections or new functionalities for existing customers using Intercom's Fin product

success_metrics.context: Critical for board reporting, regulatory compliance, and demonstrating CX program effectiveness. Used to identify at-risk customers and justify continued investment in customer experience technology.

success_metrics.metric: Cost per ticket / Cost per resolution
success_metrics.context: Essential for justifying technology investments to CFO and demonstrating efficiency gains from automation and AI. Used to build business cases for scaling operations without proportional headcount increases.

Key performance indicator for operational excellence that directly impacts customer satisfaction and competitive differentiation. Used to benchmark against industry standards and demonstrate service quality improvements.

Critical for data-driven headcount planning, budget requests, and demonstrating optimal resource allocation to board. Determines when scaling requires additional investment versus efficiency improvements.
Demonstrates ROI of AI investments and efficiency gains. Used to justify continued technology spend and quantify agent time freed for complex work. Directly tied to cost reduction goals.
Connects CX performance to revenue impact and demonstrates strategic value of customer experience investments to CEO and board. Essential for positioning CX as a revenue driver, not cost center.
Operational metric that impacts capacity planning and customer satisfaction. Used to identify process inefficiencies and opportunities for workflow automation or knowledge base improvements.

Ensures survey data is statistically valid and representative. Low response rates undermine confidence in CSAT scores when reporting to board, requiring strategic survey deployment rather than blanket sending.
Critical for regulated industries facing audits and regulatory reporting requirements. Non-compliance creates legal risk and threatens business continuity, making this a board-level concern.
Demonstrates progress toward scaling goals without headcount increases. Used to quantify impact of knowledge base and AI agent investments on reducing live agent interactions.

key_messages_to_land.message: Technology investment should drive efficiency gains that fund itself through cost reduction, not add to operational burden
key_messages_to_land.rationale: CX Executives face intense budget scrutiny and need to justify every dollar spent. They're focused on automation and scaling without headcount increases, making self-funding technology investments through efficiency the most compelling narrative.

key_messages_to_land.message: Consolidation and simplification are strategic imperatives that reduce licensing costs and improve executive visibility
key_messages_to_land.rationale: Multiple executives are actively working to consolidate fragmented instances, eliminate duplicate logins, and create unified reporting. They see operational complexity as a barrier to scaling and board-level reporting.

key_messages_to_land.message: AI creates measurable capacity without compromising quality or customer satisfaction
key_messages_to_land.rationale: Leaders need to scale operations but face resistance to headcount growth. They're looking for concrete data showing AI can handle volume while maintaining or improving CSAT, freeing agents for complex work rather than replacing them.

key_messages_to_land.message: Outcome-based pricing aligns vendor economics with customer success and reduces budget risk
key_messages_to_land.rationale: Executives are attracted to consumption-based models that tie costs to results rather than seats or features. This reduces upfront commitment risk and makes ROI easier to demonstrate to financial stakeholders.

key_messages_to_land.message: Compliance and security are non-negotiable requirements that protect both operations and executive reputation
key_messages_to_land.rationale: Regulatory requirements and security gaps create existential risk for CX leaders. Solutions must demonstrate robust compliance capabilities and prevent the legal exposure that threatens their credibility with boards.

key_messages_to_land.message: Data-driven insights transform CX from a cost center to a strategic revenue protection function
key_messages_to_land.rationale: CX Executives need to demonstrate strategic value to CEOs and boards. Connecting satisfaction data to churn prevention and revenue impact repositions customer experience as an investment, not an expense.

key_messages_to_land.message: Platform partnerships should simplify operations, not create vendor management overhead
key_messages_to_land.rationale: Executives are actively eliminating intermediaries and simplifying vendor relationships. They want direct partnerships with clear accountability and integrated solutions, not complex multi-party arrangements that add cost without value.

key_messages_to_land.message: Quick time-to-value and proven ROI are essential for securing continued investment in competitive budget cycles
key_messages_to_land.rationale: Leaders face pressure to demonstrate results quickly to justify renewal and expansion. They need concrete proof points and rapid implementation, not lengthy professional services engagements with uncertain outcomes.

product_requirements.requirement: Centralized multi-brand management with unified reporting and consolidated renewals
product_requirements.example_quote: Consolidation Goal: The primary goal is to consolidate the Rush Limbaugh instance into the main iHeartRadio instance to streamline operations, eliminate separate logins, and have a single annual renewal

product_requirements.requirement: Consumption-based AI pricing tied to automated resolutions rather than seat licenses
product_requirements.example_quote: Fin's Value Proposition: Alexander is attracted to Fin's resolution-based pricing model (per solved case) and its ease of testing with a limited budget, contrasting it with Zendesk's perceived traditional SaaS per-seat licensing

product_requirements.requirement: Integrated outbound calling and SMS with automatic ticket creation and transcription
product_requirements.example_quote: Desired Workflow: Ashley wants click-to-call functionality directly from Zendesk tickets, automatic ticket updates, and post-call transcription (within minutes) integrated into the ticket

product_requirements.requirement: Executive-level dashboards showing cost per ticket, capacity utilization, and churn correlation
product_requirements.example_quote: Workload Capacity & Workforce Management: Ashley expresses a need for data-driven insights to determine consultant workload capacity and breaking points for headcount planning

product_requirements.requirement: Advanced data protection with PII/PHI redaction and role-based access controls
product_requirements.example_quote: ADPP Introduction: Andrew and Kiran introduced ADPP, highlighting features like access logs, PII/PHI redaction, and data retention policies, asking about Sparrow's current practices and HIPAA compliance

product_requirements.requirement: Regulatory compliance reporting including centralized complaint tracking and resolution turnaround
product_requirements.example_quote: Centralized Ticketing Requirement: The BNM policy also mandates a centralized ticketing system to house all complaints, highlighting a current gap where social media channels are not yet connected to Zendesk

product_requirements.requirement: Flexible licensing that allows feature deployment to specific teams without wall-to-wall requirements
product_requirements.example_quote: Licensing Scope: Ashley wants these features only for her 18-person Solutions Hub team, not for all 100 existing Zendesk licenses across multiple teams, which presents a challenge for wall-to-wall QA licensing

product_requirements.requirement: AI analytics showing automation opportunities, volume deflection, and ROI projections
product_requirements.example_quote: Data & ROI: Zendesk can analyze Sparrow's 50,000 monthly tickets to identify automation opportunities and provide an ROI analysis for potential AI implementations

product_requirements.requirement: QA and workforce management tools that identify coaching opportunities and process improvements
product_requirements.example_quote: QA Functionality: Randy explains that QA captures and analyzes 100% of interactions (email and voice) between consultants and real estate agents, allowing for grading and identifying areas for improvement

product_requirements.requirement: Migration capabilities that preserve historical metrics and reporting for consolidated instances
product_requirements.example_quote: Metrics and Reporting Impact: Migrated tickets lose their original created/closed dates and first-time reply metrics, requiring separate reporting or custom fields for historical data, which can be a showstopper for some clients

product_requirements.requirement: CSAT deployment flexibility for different customer segments with controlled response rates
product_requirements.example_quote: CSAT Response Strategy: Ashley explains her team's goal of a 25% minimum CSAT response rate, emphasizing sending surveys after meaningful interactions rather than every email to avoid spamming

product_requirements.requirement: Knowledge base with AI-generated content and translation for multi-language support
product_requirements.example_quote: Knowledge Base Enhancements: New article editors, Gen AI writing tools (to create full articles from bullet points), and AI translation are being integrated into the standard knowledge base

information_sources.notes: CX Executives conduct hands-on evaluations of competitive solutions like Intercom Fin, RingCentral, and Salesforce, comparing features, pricing models, and ease of testing before making strategic platform decisions.

information_sources.source: Peer executive networks and industry benchmarks
information_sources.notes: Leaders reference what competitors are using and compare their performance metrics against industry standards. They seek validation that solutions work at similar scale organizations before committing.

information_sources.source: Internal finance and legal team requirements
information_sources.notes: CFOs, legal counsel, and procurement teams significantly influence platform decisions. CX Executives must align technology choices with existing vendor relationships, compliance requirements, and budget constraints set by finance.

information_sources.source: Vendor roadmaps and product roadshow events
information_sources.notes: Executives attend vendor events like Zendesk Relate to understand future capabilities and assess strategic alignment. Roadmap visibility helps them plan multi-year technology strategies and justify investments.

Partners like ACC Premier and Faye influence technology stack decisions, though executives increasingly prefer direct vendor relationships. Partner experiences with tools shape which solutions get recommended for evaluation.

information_sources.source: Customer success and account team insights
information_sources.notes: Regular syncs with vendor account teams provide executives with usage data, best practice recommendations, and peer success stories. These relationships help identify optimization opportunities and inform expansion decisions.

messaging_preferences.preference: Direct access to senior vendor leadership for strategic alignment and escalation
messaging_preferences.example_quote: Alexander's Role: Alexander Soukup is the Chief Customer Excellence Officer at Group One, responsible for customer experience and operational touchpoints for 2 million SMB customers, with Zendesk being a core platform

messaging_preferences.preference: Concrete ROI analysis with specific cost savings projections based on their data
messaging_preferences.example_quote: Data & ROI: Zendesk can analyze Sparrow's 50,000 monthly tickets to identify automation opportunities and provide an ROI analysis for potential AI implementations

messaging_preferences.preference: Quick evaluation periods with limited-scope pilots to prove value before full commitment
messaging_preferences.example_quote: Urgency for Testing: Alexander needs a solution quickly, aiming to make a decision by early next week on which AI solution to test next, given the internal pressure and previous evaluations

messaging_preferences.preference: Transparency on total cost of ownership including hidden fees and professional services
messaging_preferences.example_quote: Unincluded Costs: Randy highlights that professional services engagement and the underlying AWS Connect plumbing are not included in the current proposal

messaging_preferences.preference: Partnership framing rather than transactional vendor relationship
messaging_preferences.example_quote: Haggling Discomfort: Colin expresses discomfort with the negotiation feeling like 'haggling' and emphasizes the desire for a partnership

messaging_preferences.preference: Multi-year contracts with flexible terms that accommodate growth and changing needs
messaging_preferences.example_quote: Two-Year Contract: Luis suggests expanding to a two-year contract to help rationalize the investment and potentially secure a better rate for ARs

competitive_landscape.context: Evaluated as alternative AI solution due to resolution-based pricing and ease of testing. Attracted attention during Zendesk compliance restrictions but lacks full platform capabilities.
Evaluated for contact center capabilities and found economical with good features, but lost to Zendesk due to preference for consolidated platform and existing vendor relationship.
Major competitor in enterprise deals, particularly where procurement and IT departments prefer established enterprise platforms over specialized solutions. Represents platform lock-in risk.
Considered as underlying telephony infrastructure for contact center capabilities, particularly attractive when organizations have existing AWS commitments that can offset costs.
Direct business competitor whose voice support setup is being investigated to understand their use of AI bot functionality and benchmark against.

pain_points.description: Enterprise architects struggle with integrating Zendesk across multiple legacy systems, APIs, and data sources while maintaining data consistency and proper authentication flows. Integration complexity increases with hybrid architectures involving multiple vendors and custom middleware.
pain_points.example_quote: Data Source Challenges: Snowflake data is outdated (24-hour sync) and primarily for reporting, while the back office provides live data but lacks a unified API endpoint

pain_points.description: Technical architects face significant effort in migrating from legacy implementations to new versions, requiring careful planning around testing, configuration replication, and maintaining business continuity during transitions.
pain_points.example_quote: Migration Necessity: Zendesk is migrating internally to its new operating system, requiring Superbet to move its legacy integrations (V1) to the new Integration Builder (V2) due to unsupported old systems

pain_points.description: Technical architects encounter problems with API integrations sending placeholder values instead of null for optional parameters, and struggle with data format mismatches between systems (e.g., country code formats).
pain_points.example_quote: API Optional Parameters Issue: Francisco describes a problem where optional parameters in API integrations were sending placeholders (curly braces) instead of null values, leading to errors in their backend

pain_points.description: Architects face challenges configuring single sign-on across multiple instances and ensuring proper user authentication flows, particularly with OAuth transitions and identity management.
pain_points.example_quote: Admin Role Limitations: Rita explained that an end-user account cannot be an admin in Zendesk; only a team member can hold an admin role, which complicates their OAuth setup

pain_points.description: Technical architects struggle with maintaining proper separation between dev, QA, and production environments, ensuring API keys align correctly, and managing sandbox limitations for testing integrations.
pain_points.example_quote: Current Environment Status: The current Zendesk sandbox (ending in HH2) is designated as the Dev environment, as it already contains Dev customer data and is used by SAP PO for delta sync testing

pain_points.description: Architects need to understand how to chain API calls, store intermediate values, and retrieve data from external systems within Zendesk workflows without requiring repeated user input.
pain_points.example_quote: API Data Chaining: Maria asks how the AI can store and reuse values from one API call in subsequent calls without user intervention, to which Federico explains that saving the parameter with a unique name in the API call configuration automatically makes it available

pain_points.description: Technical architects encounter limitations with native voice capabilities, particularly around portability, SIP trunk configurations, and integrating with existing telephony infrastructure across multiple regions.
pain_points.example_quote: Problema de Portabilidad: Se confirma que no hay portabilidad de número de Uruguay para Scantec, lo que complica la integración de voz con Zendesk

pain_points.description: Architects struggle with balancing real-time data requirements against batch sync limitations, particularly when source systems have 24-hour delays but business needs require current information.
pain_points.example_quote: Snowflake Data Timeliness: Amy highlights that while the back office is ideal for real-time data, 90% of customers don't raise tickets within 24 hours of booking, making Snowflake potentially acceptable for routing

goals.description: Architects want to create seamless integrations between Zendesk and backend systems (CRM, ERP, custom applications) that enable real-time data flow and automated workflows without manual intervention.
goals.example_quote: Recommended Solution: The preferred solution is to get up-to-date information directly from the back office via API, as it supports both use cases effectively

goals.description: Technical architects aim to implement sophisticated automation that can intelligently route tickets, trigger appropriate API calls, and orchestrate multi-step processes based on business logic.
goals.example_quote: Automation Objective: The customer (Hizar) wants to automate the first response to incoming tickets, including assigning severity, acknowledging the site, and determining if it's a production or non-production issue, to eliminate manual agent intervention

goals.description: Architects seek to build solutions that can scale across multiple regions, business units, or subsidiaries while maintaining consistency and avoiding redundant configuration work.
goals.example_quote: Expansión Regional: La operación inicial es para Uruguay, pero Scantec tiene presencia en otros cuatro países de Latinoamérica, lo que representa una oportunidad de expansión para otros canales

goals.description: Enterprise architects want to implement full ITSM capabilities including CMDB, change control, problem management, and asset management to meet enterprise IT requirements.
goals.example_quote: ITSM Requirements: Ben highlights the need for full ITSM capabilities for IT, including CMDB, change control, problem management, asset management, effective self-service, and chatbot integration, which is a significant transformation

goals.description: Architects want to implement solutions following best practices that can serve as templates for future use cases and demonstrate proper methodology to the organization.
goals.example_quote: Proposed POC Flow: Jose Eduardo proposes using Federico's almost-complete flow for the Proof of Concept (POC) testing, highlighting its adherence to best practices and its potential as a guide for future use cases

goals.description: Technical architects aim to understand when and how to deploy multiple Zendesk instances for different departments or business needs while managing licensing efficiently.
goals.example_quote: Zendesk Instances: Rodrigo explains that an instance is a separated logical and physical configuration, data, and access environment. Licenses can be split across multiple instances

objections.objection: Concerns about migration effort and timeline impact on business operations, particularly for complex integrations with multiple dependencies
objections.example_quote: Current Tech Stack & Issues: Moultrie uses Zendesk Talk, has technical debt, relies on middleware (Zapier, Poly AI), and is transitioning to Quick for AI IVR, while awaiting promised SIP out functionality. Past Engagement & Concern: A previous POC with Zendesk's Ultimate AI flopped, and Moultrie's main concern is the effort required to migrate from Talk to Contact Center

objections.objection: Uncertainty about technical feasibility of specific integration requirements, particularly with complex authentication flows and data transformations
objections.example_quote: BP ID Necessity: Hybris APIs require a Business Partner ID (BP ID) and order number; the BP ID needs to be retrieved from the external user ID (GUID) for successful API calls

objections.objection: Risk of downstream impacts when making configuration changes, particularly around tag deletion, trigger modifications, or API updates affecting existing automations
objections.example_quote: Tag Deletion Impact: Hizar wants to delete 317 unused tags, but Rohit warned of potential downstream impacts on existing triggers, automations, and macros, suggesting a risk assessment and approval from Lorraine (customer's manager) before proceeding

objections.objection: Concerns about Zendesk's capabilities for project management and document storage compared to alternatives like Atlassian
objections.example_quote: Zendesk's Limitations: Kyle and Devyn acknowledge that Zendesk is not a document repository or a sophisticated project management tool like Monday, but they can address some PM use cases

objections.objection: Sandbox limitations preventing proper testing and configuration validation before production deployment
objections.example_quote: Access Issue: Joanna cannot review the procedure beforehand because they don't have live access to the client's system, which uses single sign-on, unlike their sandbox

success_metrics.context: Technical architects need to ensure integrations perform within acceptable latency thresholds and maintain high availability for business-critical workflows

product_requirements.requirement: Support for complex API orchestration with conditional branching, error handling, and the ability to chain multiple API calls with intermediate data storage

product_requirements.requirement: Robust handling of optional API parameters, allowing null values instead of placeholders and supporting various data format transformations

product_requirements.requirement: Support for multiple Zendesk instances with clear separation between environments and the ability to replicate configurations efficiently

product_requirements.requirement: Comprehensive migration tools that handle both automatic and manual migration paths with proper testing capabilities
product_requirements.example_quote: Migration Approaches: Two options are discussed: an automatic swap using a migration tool (requires identical scenario counts, instantaneous, no testing window) or a manual update of dialogues (more control, allows testing, but time-consuming). A hybrid approach is suggested

product_requirements.requirement: Real-time data access capabilities with fallback to batch sync when needed, supporting both push and pull data models

product_requirements.requirement: Flexible SIP trunk integration options for voice that support multiple carriers, regions, and existing telephony infrastructure

pain_points.description: Global operations leaders experience delays in issue resolution and lack proactive support from vendor account teams, particularly during critical periods like renewals or product transitions.
pain_points.example_quote: Orla feels there hasn't been sufficient support from Zendesk to resolve the Co-pilot issue, despite raising it early and repeatedly

pain_points.description: Organizations are not leveraging advanced capabilities of their existing platforms, operating with basic ticketing functionality while missing optimization opportunities due to lack of strategic guidance.
pain_points.example_quote: Arista is currently using Zendesk in a 'very amateurish' and 'poor fashion,' not fully leveraging its capabilities, and seeks Zendesk's help to make smarter use of the tool

pain_points.description: Operations leaders struggle with agent count discrepancies, unclear charging models, and inability to validate billing data, creating internal approval challenges and budget uncertainty.
pain_points.example_quote: Orla disputes Zendesk's reported agent numbers (around 194-200) versus her internal count (165), noting an unexplained 'spike' in usage from October without corresponding new hires

pain_points.description: Unexpected product deprecations with insufficient transition time create operational disruption and force rapid evaluation of replacement features without adequate information for decision-making.
pain_points.example_quote: Zendesk is decommissioning the current 'Flow Builder' (chat flow) by August/end of year, which is a fundamental change for BoyleSports as it currently contains 30-45% of their traffic

pain_points.description: Operations teams rely on manual processes for routing, data extraction, and quality assurance due to lack of automation maturity, limiting scalability and creating consistency risks.
pain_points.example_quote: Agents manually pick tickets from queues rather than having them automatically routed, though some limited auto-assignment to groups exists for specific functions like payroll

pain_points.description: Workflows span multiple systems requiring manual data combination for reporting, with ticket systems not containing complete context, complicating operational visibility and decision-making.
pain_points.example_quote: Meghana explained that for some teams (e.g., contracts), Zendesk is not the complete workflow, as tickets often transition to other systems (like Ironclad), requiring manual data combination for reports

pain_points.description: When operational issues arise, lack of real-time diagnostic data and root cause analysis capabilities prevents rapid resolution and creates uncertainty about system reliability.
pain_points.example_quote: Orla from BoyleSports confirms seeing live delays of 15-20 seconds, and the 30-second incident was not an isolated attempt to reproduce an issue, but a real occurrence impacting their operations

pain_points.description: Standard reporting tools focus on basic metrics rather than operational efficiency insights, requiring manual exports and external BI tools to answer specific business questions.
pain_points.example_quote: Matthew uses Zendesk Explorer for agent reports but finds it focuses on SLAs/satisfaction, desiring more granular data on agent efficiency

pain_points.description: Administrative tasks like field management, form configuration, and system maintenance require excessive manual effort and technical expertise relative to team size.
pain_points.example_quote: Meghana highlighted the complexity for admins in managing fields (e.g., suppressing outdated ones) and the need for more advanced form functionalities like currency changes, while avoiding custom code that is hard to sustain

pain_points.description: Vendor announcements lack clarity, sufficient lead time, and actionable details, forcing operations teams to seek clarification and creating uncertainty about feature impact.
pain_points.example_quote: Virginia expressed frustration with recent Zendesk announcements being unclear and lacking sufficient lead time, a sentiment Julia's manager also shares

goals.description: Operations leaders prioritize maximizing team productivity, automating repetitive work, and improving resource allocation to handle growing demand without proportional headcount increases.
goals.example_quote: Jude's Core Goals: Meghana stated their primary goals are providing better service, capturing demand to optimize resources and automate manual work, rather than solely focusing on SLAs

goals.description: Achieving smarter, automated ticket assignment based on skills, context, and priority to reduce manual triage and improve first-contact resolution.
goals.example_quote: Arista seeks a smarter, more automated way to assign tickets, particularly within a group where different agents handle different types of issues

goals.description: Maintaining predictable operational costs with transparent usage models that align with actual consumption patterns and allow for future planning without surprise charges.
goals.example_quote: Dave suggests moving from 'agent months' to a 'named agents' model, potentially with headroom, as BoyleSports' usage patterns don't align with the seasonal flexibility 'agent months' is designed for

goals.description: Implementing AI capabilities to handle routine inquiries, provide self-service answers, and reduce agent workload while maintaining quality of service.
goals.example_quote: Niraj is building a case for upgrading the Zendesk instance to leverage AI capabilities and agent functionality

goals.description: Gaining granular visibility into operational performance, agent efficiency, and workflow bottlenecks through customized reporting that tells specific stories for different stakeholders.
goals.example_quote: Meghana emphasized the need for reports that tell specific stories for different leaders and teams, rather than generic dashboards

goals.description: Connecting support systems with HR, finance, and business tools to enable automated data flow, reduce manual work, and provide complete workflow visibility.
goals.example_quote: Alexis suggested integrating Workday to pull manager, region, and cost center information for routing, noting it's an out-of-the-box integration requiring security approval

goals.description: Establishing scalable quality assurance processes that maintain service standards across global teams without requiring extensive manual review.
goals.example_quote: Virginia proposes creating a separate, unweighted Auto QA scorecard to run autonomously on all tickets for insight gathering and AI coaching

goals.description: Gaining the ability to independently modify workflows, forms, and business rules without requiring vendor professional services or deep technical expertise.
goals.example_quote: Val confirms a configuration workbook will be provided to empower Starlims to make modifications independently, reducing reliance on professional services

goals.description: Solving customer problems on the first interaction by providing agents with better context, tools, and knowledge to avoid escalations and repeat contacts.
goals.example_quote: Matthew emphasized 'getting it right' and solving problems the first time

goals.description: Developing collaborative relationships with vendors who provide strategic guidance, proactive support, and joint problem-solving rather than reactive issue resolution.
goals.example_quote: Niraj aims to discuss upgrading the Zendesk platform after the current salary compensation review process, seeking Zendesk's partnership in building a business case

key_messages_to_land.message: We provide strategic partnership, not just transaction support
key_messages_to_land.rationale: Operations leaders are frustrated with reactive, insufficient vendor support during critical periods. They seek vendors who proactively help them optimize, build business cases, and resolve issues before renewals, not just respond to tickets.

key_messages_to_land.message: You unlock value already in your subscription without requiring upgrades
key_messages_to_land.rationale: Many organizations are dramatically underutilizing their current platforms. Operations leaders want guidance on maximizing existing investments before being sold additional products, demonstrating vendor commitment to their success over revenue expansion.

key_messages_to_land.message: Transparent, predictable pricing aligned to your business model
key_messages_to_land.rationale: Billing discrepancies, unexpected charges, and opaque usage models create approval friction. Operations leaders need pricing that matches their actual consumption patterns and allows confident budget forecasting for executive stakeholders.

key_messages_to_land.message: Automation reduces costs, but ROI must be clear and incremental
key_messages_to_land.rationale: Operations leaders see AI and automation as efficiency drivers but resist new charges for capabilities that replace existing free functionality. They need to see net savings or clear capacity gains, not cost shifts.

key_messages_to_land.message: Self-sufficiency through configuration control and training
key_messages_to_land.rationale: Operations leaders want to independently modify workflows and optimize systems without professional services dependencies. Empowering them with tools, training, and documentation builds long-term partnership and reduces friction.

key_messages_to_land.message: Product transitions require adequate lead time and information for stakeholder alignment
key_messages_to_land.rationale: Forced migrations without demonstrations, clear documentation, or sufficient transition windows prevent operations leaders from securing internal approval and create operational risk they cannot defend to executives.

key_messages_to_land.message: Integration maturity determines operational efficiency at scale
key_messages_to_land.rationale: Operations leaders managing global teams need seamless data flow between systems to eliminate manual work. Deep integrations with HR, finance, and business systems are operational requirements, not nice-to-haves.

key_messages_to_land.message: Granular analytics drive operational decisions, not just executive dashboards
key_messages_to_land.rationale: Operations leaders need detailed efficiency metrics, agent-level performance data, and customized views for different stakeholders. Generic SLA dashboards do not answer their specific operational questions or support improvement initiatives.

key_messages_to_land.message: Quality consistency scales through intelligent automation, not manual review
key_messages_to_land.rationale: Operations leaders managing hundreds of agents globally cannot rely on manual QA processes. They need automated quality monitoring that maintains standards while freeing managers to focus on coaching and strategic improvements.

key_messages_to_land.message: Performance diagnostics and root cause visibility prevent operational disruption
key_messages_to_land.rationale: When issues arise, operations leaders need real-time data and clear root cause analysis to rapidly resolve problems and defend system reliability to executives. Delayed or unclear diagnostics undermine confidence in the platform.
